game_name: Earth 2150: Escape from the Blue Planet

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core loop established immediately; players must balance resource retention vs. evacuation commitment from first mission
Scripted encounters introduce unit types and engagement mechanics progressively through campaign
Adds supply line vulnerability; enables unit rushing but increases base interdependency
Players learn to build specialized unit classes; ammo logistics and weapon trade-offs emerge
Gimmick weapons with unique mechanics (e.g., laser heat-detonation); require tactical rethinking

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Scripted at mission start across campaign
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: World thaws from ice → plants grow → deserts overtake → lava flows; affects weapon effectiveness and unit availability (navies phased out)
Situational deployment; actions change based on environmental state

element_introduction_order.element: Scripted events (alliance falling apart, hacker unit capture)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dynamic objective changes force tactical adaptation mid-mission
Limit increases per mission; forces player to choose between building locally vs. transporting from main base

typical_relief_triggers: Successful enemy base destruction (all forces eliminated)
Completing mission objective (resource target, defense survival)
Transitioning to main base for unit/research management
Spaceport auto-launch of 10k credits (irreversible progress toward evacuation)
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy scout units arrive at player base
Supply line cut (landing pad destroyed or damaged)
Resource depletion before evacuation quota met
Scripted alliance breakup or unit capture event
Ammo depletion on critical units (logistics failure)
Population limit reached; cannot build new units
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-form RTS tension with persistent meta-goal (1M credits evacuation). Individual missions range from skirmish (quick elimination) to strategic holdout (defend ambassador). Global campaign time limit is generous, allowing tactical deliberation but maintaining endgame pressure. Environmental shifts introduce periodic rhythm changes (temperature affects weapon balance, water loss removes naval units).

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Main base can be freely switched to/from mission map; persistent parallel space rewards exploration of unit customization and research labs

difficulty_curve.early_game: Mission 1 establishes resource gathering and evacuation meta-loop in low-threat setting. Basic unit types introduced. Player learns rhythm of ore → credits → units → spaceport delivery.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy force scaling increases; scripted events (alliance collapse, hacker captures) add dynamic objective shifts. Landing pads become critical vulnerability. Unit customization depth required to counter specialized enemy types. Energy weapons introduce counter-play mechanics.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Environmental degradation (desert, lava, evaporated seas) forces obsolescence of unit types and weapon strategies. Heat-based weapons shift effectiveness. Superweapon options change. Player must manage population limits while maintaining military edge and evacuation progress.
notable_spikes: First combat encounter (introduction of enemy scaling)
Landing pad destruction (supply line vulnerability first experienced)
Environmental shift transitions (weapon/navy obsolescence forces rebuild)
Late-game resource scarcity as temperature rises and terrain shifts limit ore deposits

notable_level_patterns.description: Main base persists through entire campaign; acts as secure rear-area for long-term unit accumulation, research, and credit management. Can be switched to at will, breaking mission pacing. Units built here transport to mission via air carrier.

Ore gathered in mission can either be spent on local units/buildings (faster but abandoned) or committed to evacuation quota (slower but counts toward 1M credit goal). Creates strategic tension every mission.
Landing pad and ammo supply carriers create exploitable choke points. Enemy can focus air defense on supply routes. Adds tactical layer beyond standard base-and-conquer RTS.
Defend mission becomes assault mission when alliance collapses. Hacker steals units. Events trigger at scripted times, forcing tactical reassessment mid-engagement.

Terrain scripted to change at mission start. Frigid → thaw → growth → desert → lava. Changes weapon effectiveness, removes navies, alters superweapon capabilities. Forces player to adapt strategies across campaign arc.
